families.name: Cuprates
families.description: Layered copper oxides; the core ARPES targets are Fermi arcs, pseudogap, d-wave gap, nodal/antinodal dichotomy, and q-dependent charge order.

families.name: Iron-based
families.description: Fe pnictides/chalcogenides; ARPES often tracks multiband Fermi surfaces, orbital-selective behavior, nematicity, and superconducting gap anisotropy.

families.name: Nickelates
families.description: Ni-based superconductors; a growing ARPES target for comparing cuprate-like and multiorbital physics.

families.name: Conventional / BCS
families.description: Elemental and intermetallic superconductors; useful Tc and gap calibration references.

families.name: Transition-metal dichalcogenides
families.description: Layered TMD superconductors; ARPES focuses on CDW, valley physics, Fermi surface nesting, and gap opening.

families.name: Heavy fermion
families.description: Strongly correlated f-electron superconductors; ARPES targets heavy bands and hybridization.

families.name: Hydrides / high pressure
families.description: High-pressure superconductors with high Tc; not usually routine ARPES because pressure cells limit photoemission.

materials.photon_arpes_notes: Useful as a historical reference, not a common modern ARPES target.
materials.source_note: Kamerlingh Onnes discovered superconductivity in mercury in 1911.

materials.photon_arpes_notes: Bulk metal; matrix elements depend strongly on photon energy.
materials.source_note: Representative well-known elemental Tc; verify exact sample value before publication.
Representative well-known elemental Tc; verify exact sample value before publication.

materials.photon_arpes_notes: Harder surface preparation than layered materials.
materials.source_note: Representative A15 superconductor; verify exact Tc and synthesis record before publication.

materials.photon_arpes_notes: Photon energy can separate sigma and pi band weight.
materials.source_note: Discovery of superconductivity near 39 K in MgB2 was reported in 2001.

materials.tc_note: First high-Tc cuprate family reported by Bednorz and Muller.
materials.photon_arpes_notes: Polarization and photon energy help separate orbital character.
materials.source_note: Bednorz and Muller discovered high-temperature superconductivity in Ba-La-Cu-O in 1986.

materials.tc_note: High-Tc cuprate above liquid nitrogen temperature.
materials.photon_arpes_notes: Surface polarity and chain states require careful photon-energy/polarization choices.
materials.source_note: YBCO around 92-93 K was reported in 1987.

materials.tc_note: Tc varies with doping; often near 90-95 K around optimal doping.
materials.photon_arpes_notes: Matrix elements and superstructure replicas are major ARPES concerns.
materials.source_note: Template entry; doping-specific Tc and synthesis source should be verified for each dataset.

materials.photon_arpes_notes: Photon energy maps kz and separates 3D hole pockets.
materials.source_note: Ba1-xKxFe2As2 with x around 0.4 was reported superconducting up to 38 K in 2008.

materials.tc_note: Bulk FeSe around 8-9 K; monolayer/interface values differ.
materials.photon_arpes_notes: Low photon energy laser ARPES is often useful for small Fermi pockets.
materials.source_note: Bulk FeSe Tc is sample dependent; monolayer FeSe/STO should be tracked separately.

materials.tc_note: Approximate thin-film scale; strongly sample and synthesis dependent.
materials.photon_arpes_notes: Thin films and polar surfaces make photon-energy/polarization control important.
materials.source_note: Template nickelate entry; use publication-specific Tc and synthesis details for curated data.

materials.tc_note: High pressure; value depends on pressure/sample and is not routine ARPES-accessible.
materials.photon_arpes_notes: High-pressure state is difficult for standard ARPES; ambient/surface electronic structure may differ.
materials.source_note: Template high-pressure nickelate entry; verify pressure-specific data before use.

materials.photon_arpes_notes: Photon energy helps separate kz dispersion and surface/bulk contributions.
materials.source_note: Representative layered TMD; verify sample-specific Tc and CDW vector before publication.

materials.photon_arpes_notes: Resonant photon energies can enhance f-electron spectral weight.
materials.source_note: Template heavy-fermion entry; verify sample-specific details before publication.

materials.tc_note: High pressure; not a routine ARPES material.
materials.photon_arpes_notes: Diamond-anvil pressure environment prevents standard ARPES.
materials.source_note: Included for superconductivity history context; not suitable for ordinary ARPES workflow.
